mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals weak labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite high unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives equity valuations higher through improved corporate revenue prospects over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to predict positive returns in > 70% of cases, or their combined IC drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 105 signals weak supply chain activity that reduces corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite low INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL > 265) during a monetary tightening cycle suppresses equity valuations by increasing real interest rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 265.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job growth (PAYEMS > 144,000) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances when PAYEMS exceeds 144,000.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real sales (RSAFS > 619,000) reflects strong consumer demand that lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances when RSAFS exceeds 619,000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 0.1%) and inflation (CPIAUCSL > 265) signal a tightening monetary environment that raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL exceed thresholds.
